visits.text: Holy Mountain (Svatá Hora) has watched over Příbram for more than three centuries. This Baroque complex is one of Bohemia's great Marian pilgrimage sites; it bears the title of minor basilica. At its center, a Gothic statue of the Virgin, carved according to legend from pear wood. A long covered stairway climbs there from the town.

Opened in 1886, the Mining Museum of Příbram (Hornické muzeum) is the largest of its kind in the country. Installed in the buildings of the Březové Hory mines, it unrolls the silver-lead past of one of the major mining towns of the old Austro-Hungarian Empire. Extraction ceased in 1978. Several circuits; budget generously if you want to do everything—the site is extensive.

activities.text: The little mine train at Březové Hory plunges underground, hard hat required. The museum offers a real immersion: underground galleries stretching several kilometers, a slide down to a giant water wheel, old steam machines. Kids love it. Bring warm clothes—it's chilly underground—and book the guided descents that leave in groups.

days_plan.itin_title: 7 Days Around Prague: Kutná Hora, Castles & Karst

visits.text: Příhrazské skály form a labyrinth of 170 sandstone towers east of Mnichovo Hradiště. Their highlight: Drábské světničky, rock chambers carved into the sandstone in the 15th century by fleeing Hussites, whose walls still bear traces of this use. The nature reserve was classified in 1999 over 520 hectares between 255 and 463 meters altitude.

activities.text: Start from Dneboh parking (735 meters from the reserve entrance) on the red-marked trail toward Drábské světničky and the Na krásné vyhlídce viewpoint - view over the region from the summit of Mužský (463 m). Allow 2h for the main loop. Terrain not suitable for strollers.

days_plan.itin_title: Northern Bohemia: From the Elbe to the Rocks of Bohemian Paradise

places.name: Primda

visits.text: Mentioned in 1121 in Cosmas's chronicle, Přimda Castle is the oldest known stone castle in Bohemia. Built illegally by Germans, it was immediately seized by Prince Vladislav I. The granite ashlar residential tower preserves a rare Romanesque garderobe. From the summit (848 m), clear view to Germany.

activities.text: Allow thirty minutes' walk through the forest from the village parking lot. An educational trail of 7 km with 12 stations starts from Přimda church. Guided tours are offered by reservation via hrad-primda.cz.

days_plan.itin_title: West Bohemia in 10 days: Spas, Castles & Beer

map_keywords: Přimda Castle oldest stone castle Bohemia

visits.text: You don't come to Ratibořice to visit a castle. You come to inhabit a novel: Grandmother's Valley, Babiččino údolí, is the setting of 'Babička' by Božena Němcová (1855), one of the most read texts in Czech literature—and the author's face graces the 500 CZK banknote.

A discreet château in a bucolic valley, and yet: in 1813, Metternich and Tsar Alexander I met here to seal the coalition against Napoleon. The Duchess of Zaháň, owner of the premises, had the habit of gathering European aristocracy in her classicist salons, rebuilt in 1825-1826 on Italian plans.

activities.text: Follow on foot the Babiččino údolí trail from the château to the Staré Bělidlo (3 km one way): the Rudr Mill, the water mangle, the half-timbered house of the old bleachery, and Otto Gutfreund's sculpture group 'Grandmother and Grandchildren' punctuate the walk. Total duration: 1h30 round trip.

days_plan.itin_title: Northeast Bohemia: From the Giant Mountains to the Rock Cities, 9 Days

visits.text: Perched on a promontory the Vltava wraps around on three sides, Rožmberk Castle ranks among the oldest in Bohemia, mentioned as early as 1253. Cradle of the powerful Rožmberk family, it passed to the Buquoys in 1620. The widest view opens from the English Tower, at the cost of more than 200 steps. Open April to October, guided tours in English.

activities.text: The borough of Rožmberk nad Vltavou deserves a descent to wander after the castle: 400 inhabitants, picturesque houses, a few cafés by the water. A former residence now houses a hotel-restaurant. Small, peaceful, quickly explored.

days_plan.itin_title: South Bohemia: Český Krumlov, Castles & Ponds in 10 Days

visits.text: Founded in 1032, the Sázava Monastery (Sázavský klášter) was a rare hub of Slavonic culture, where scribes wrote in Old Church Slavonic rather than Latin. St. Procopius, its founder, remains the guardian figure. The complex blends Romanesque, Gothic, and Baroque remnants at the river's edge. Its curious treasure: a "Madonna of Sázava" where the Virgin seems to scold the Child. Mostly guided tours; check off-season hours.

activities.text: Push on to the Sázava glassmaking center after the monastery. The town preserves an old glass tradition, and the František glassworks opens its workshop-gallery to visitors: you watch blowing and cutting and leave with a piece if the mood strikes. It's artisanal, not industrial. Pair it with a short stroll along the Sázava riverbanks, pleasant in good weather.

days_plan.itin_title: 7 Days Around Prague: Kutná Hora, Castles & Karst

activities.text: Settle onto a beach at Lake Slapy in warm weather. It's Prague's leisure reservoir: swimming, paddle boats, launches, and hundreds of wooden cottages (the chata) clinging to wooded slopes. The atmosphere is family-friendly, no frills. The water warms up properly only in July-August; outside summer, stick to the views—swimming stings.

days_plan.itin_title: 7 Days Around Prague: Kutná Hora, Castles & Karst

visits.text: A white arched bridge over the nascent Elbe, chalets packed into a narrow valley, a torrent roaring below: Špindlerův Mlýn announces itself before you even arrive. The main ski resort of the Krkonoše is admittedly touristy, but the natural setting makes up for everything—and in summer, the ski lifts serve as a springboard for hikers.

The Pančava Waterfall, with its 148 meters of drop in four tiers (36, 39, 23, and 20 m), is the highest in the Czech Republic. It falls into the Labský důl from the Pančava plateau. You can reach it from the Medvědín cable car upper station in about 45 minutes on foot.

The Labský Waterfall once had a peculiarity that guides no longer mention: the former owner of the Labská bouda had built a small dam above the precipice. When tourists gathered, he would open the valve to make the spectacle more impressive. The dam has disappeared—for ecological reasons.

The highest in the Czech Republic and yet almost invisible from below: the slope of the Labský důl hides the Pančava Waterfall until the last turn. You have to climb to the Medvědín station to see it plunge 148 meters into the valley.

Four tiers—36, 39, 23, and 20 meters—make up the total drop from the Pančava meadow. The flow varies greatly by season: impressive during snowmelt, reduced in summer. Below, the Labský Waterfall (45 m) offers a second stop on the same descent route.

activities.text: Take the Medvědín cable car up, then descend on foot through the Labský důl valley (11 km blue-marked trail, 2h30 easy walk): you'll pass the Pančava Waterfall seen from below, then the Labský Waterfall, before rejoining Špindlerův Mlýn along the river.

If your legs can handle a full day, continue from Labská bouda to Pramen Labe, the symbolic source of the Elbe, 1 km further: a stone well, coats of arms of cities the river crosses to the North Sea, a wooden sculpture. The actual source area is closed to the public to protect the vegetation.

From the Medvědín cable car upper station, follow the red-marked trail toward Pančavská louka: 45 minutes one way, well-visible markers. Return by the same path or continue to Labská bouda to join the Labský důl circuit.

days_plan.itin_title: Northeast Bohemia: From the Giant Mountains to the Rock Cities, 9 Days
Northeast Bohemia: From the Giant Mountains to the Rock Cities, 9 Days
Bohemia: Prague, sandstone cities & thermal spas in 14 days

visits.text: The old stone bridge spans the Mže at Stříbro, a former mining town whose name means 'silver'. The historic center preserves some Renaissance houses and a Dominican convent. Quiet town, ideal for a lunch stop.

activities.text: The Amati factory, which produces musical instruments in Stříbro, is known to brass players worldwide. If the shop is open, have a look. The Saturday morning market on the main square offers local fruits and vegetables.

days_plan.itin_title: West Bohemia in 10 days: Spas, Castles & Beer

visits.text: Granite pillars rise above the Ohře like a petrified procession. The Svatošské Rocks, a national natural monument since 1933, owe their name to the legend of Jan Svatoš: a groom turned to stone by the devil. The columns reach about fifty meters. Goethe sketched them in his notebooks.

activities.text: Follow the trail from Loket along the Ohře (6 km, easy) or come by car via Doubí. A rustic restaurant with a terrace on the river offers a rest stop. The rocks are also used by climbers.

days_plan.itin_title: West Bohemia in 10 days: Spas, Castles & Beer

visits.text: Water surrounds Švihov Castle on all sides. This Gothic fortress, built from 1480 by Půta Švihovský in the Úhlava meadows, was protected by two moats and two enclosures. The Habsburgs ordered its demolition after the Thirty Years' War, but the work dragged on and the castle survived. It was then converted into a granary, which paradoxically preserved it from modifications.

Czechs know it mainly as the setting for the film Tři oříšky pro Popelku (Three Wishes for Cinderella, 1973), a Christmas classic in Central Europe. A 'Cinderella' themed trail connects filming locations around the castle. The Gothic chapel and the ballroom with its painted Renaissance ceiling are the highlights of the interior visit.

activities.text: Rent a rowboat to circle the water-filled moat. The ride lasts about fifteen minutes and offers a unique perspective on the bastions. On-site rental in season.

days_plan.itin_title: West Bohemia in 10 days: Spas, Castles & Beer

visits.text: Sgraffito, arcaded gables, and a fountain from 1567: Žižka Square is one of the finest Renaissance squares in Bohemia, under the gaze of Hussite leader Jan Žižka's statue. Everything radiates from here. The old Gothic town hall on the west side houses the Hussite Museum.

Descend into the Hussite underground beneath the square: half a kilometer of 15th-century galleries, up to 16 meters below ground, dug for storage and shelter. The Hussite Museum above tells the story of "Tábor, fortress of the righteous" and displays a treasure of some 4,000 silver coins. Closed Mondays, combined ticket with Kotnov Tower.

The oldest remnants of Tábor are here: Kotnov Tower and Bechyně Gate, from the 13th century, predating the Hussites. 155 steps lead to the top of the tower, 25 meters up, with views over the rooftops, Jordán lake, and the hills. Modest entrance fee, the neighboring gate holds an exhibit on the medieval town.

activities.text: Tábor's real pleasure is getting lost in the tangle of narrow alleys, deliberately laid out to hinder attackers. For a playful break, the Chocolate and Marzipan Museum delights children. Some good terraces around the square.

days_plan.itin_title: South Bohemia: Český Krumlov, Castles & Ponds in 10 Days

visits.text: The Bohemian Forest Museum in Tachov occupies a former Franciscan convent. The exhibitions cover the nature of the Bohemian Forest, frontier life, and villages that disappeared after 1945. The neighboring château and remains of the town walls can be visited in passing. Quiet stopover town, no crowds.

Don't miss the convent refectory, where a life-size trompe-l'oeil supper and biblical frescoes cover the walls and ceiling. The building dates from 1686-1689, designed by architect Martin Allio. Access included in the museum ticket.

activities.text: Climb the 150 steps of the Vysoká lookout tower on the hill above town. The panorama embraces the entire Bohemian Forest to the Bavarian border. Free access, allow 20 minutes round-trip from the center.

days_plan.itin_title: West Bohemia in 10 days: Spas, Castles & Beer

places.name: Telc

visits.text: Zachariáš of Hradec Square is one of Europe's best-preserved Renaissance squares. Its elongated triangle shape is ringed by arcaded houses with unique pastel facades, all rebuilt after a devastating 1530 fire. Zachariáš of Hradec, returning from a trip to Genoa in 1551 captivated by the Italian Renaissance, undertook to transform Telč into a model town with Italian master architects. Inscribed on UNESCO's World Heritage List since 1992, this historic center is considered the most perfect example of Italian Renaissance art north of the Alps.

Telč Castle, transformed from a Gothic fortress into a Renaissance residence beginning in 1553, is one of the Czech Republic's best-preserved Renaissance castles. The Great Golden Hall, extending the entire length of one wing, possesses an extraordinary carved coffered ceiling from the 16th century. Construction was partly financed by the mining dowry of Zachariáš's first wife, Kateřina of Valdštejn.
Climb the 153 steps of St. James Church tower for panoramic views over the square and the three ponds encircling the town—medieval reservoirs that once served as natural moats and fish farms.

activities.text: Walk or cycle the trails along the ponds for shifting perspectives of Renaissance facades reflected in the water.

map_keywords: Zachariáš of Hradec Square Telč náměstí Zachariáše z Hradce
St. James Church Tower Telč Kostel svatého Jakuba
Church of the Holy Spirit Telč Kostel Jména Ježíš

visits.text: Founded in 1193 by the Blessed Hroznata, Teplá Monastery is one of the oldest Premonstratensian monasteries in Bohemia. The Romanesque Basilica of the Annunciation, consecrated in 1232 in the presence of King Wenceslas I, has spanned eight centuries. The monastery served as a barracks under the communist regime before being returned to the Premonstratensians in 1990.

The monastery library impresses as much as Strahov in Prague, in a more intimate setting. The collection of portraits of all abbots since the 12th century is complete. The park along the Teplá River, with its Lourdes grotto and modern Stations of the Cross (2016), takes about half an hour to walk.

days_plan.itin_title: West Bohemia in 10 days: Spas, Castles & Beer

visits.text: Less famous than Adršpach, judged more spectacular by most hikers who do both. The blue loop through the Teplice Rocks crosses 6 km of taller gorges, with clear views from the ridges. The two sites are connected by the Vlk gorge (2.5 km)—can be combined in a long day.

Fifteen minutes from the entrance, a metal staircase of 300 steps climbs to the ruined Strmen castle and a panoramic view of the entire massif. A portion of the trail is closed in April and May during peregrine falcon nesting—check before coming.

activities.text: Start early on the Teplice circuit: by midday the parking overflows and the gorges fill up. Bring water and a snack—nothing is sold inside the site. Entry about 100 CZK, parking 100 CZK.

days_plan.itin_title: Northeast Bohemia: From the Giant Mountains to the Rock Cities, 9 Days

visits.text: Founded in the 18th century for Empress Maria Theresa, Terezín was a military fortress. The Nazis turned it into a transit ghetto from 1941 to 1945 for Jews from across occupied Europe. The national memorial inaugurated in 1947 comprises the Ghetto Museum (in the former municipal school), the Small Fortress-Gestapo prison, the crematorium, and the national cemetery. Allow at least half a day for the complete visit.

In the Small Fortress, the cells are so cramped and the walls so low that you physically grasp the notion of overcrowding. A former Gestapo prison, it's now visited with a guide included in the ticket. Prisoners' inscriptions and graffiti remain visible on the walls. The combined ticket (about 220 Kč) covers the Small Fortress and the Ghetto Museum and is valid for 4 consecutive days.

The Ghetto Museum is essential, even for those familiar with the history. The exhibition in the former school presents drawings by deported children, saved by their teacher. The Magdeburg Barracks (open April-October) recreates a dormitory as deportees lived it. About 34,000 people died in Terezín itself - primarily from exhaustion and diseases linked to overcrowding.

activities.text: Allow at least half a day: combined tickets include the Small Fortress, the Ghetto Museum, and the cemetery. A guided tour (available from Prague, about 5 hours total) adds valuable context. The memorial is open daily except December 24, 25, and 26.

days_plan.itin_title: Northern Bohemia: From the Elbe to the Rocks of Bohemian Paradise
Bohemia: Prague, sandstone cities & thermal spas in 14 days

visits.text: The sandstone formations at Tisá crunch underfoot like candy - and the formations bear names that say it all: the Elephant, the Tortoise, the Gate of Africa. Tiské stěny (Tisá Walls) formed about 90 million years ago at the bottom of a shallow Cretaceous sea. Protected as a nature reserve since 1996, they constitute the westernmost part of the Elbe Sandstone Formations, at the edge of Bohemian Switzerland National Park.

activities.text: Choose the long circuit (about 5 km) to reach Skalní náměstí, the rock square where both routes meet in a figure-eight. Both routes are doable in about 2 hours. Paid entry depending on which side you start from (about 30 Kč). Best period is April to October. Parking 100 Kč.

days_plan.itin_title: Northern Bohemia: From the Elbe to the Rocks of Bohemian Paradise

visits.text: Třebíč's Jewish Quarter is the only Jewish monument listed as UNESCO World Heritage outside Israel (2003). Developed from the 12th century, it comprises 123 houses, two synagogues, and a cemetery with 4,000 graves. Its particularity is remarkable: unlike other European ghettos, coexistence with the Christian community here was exceptionally peaceful. In 1723, when a law imposed the creation of closed ghettos, Christian residents living in the territory voluntarily exchanged their houses with their Jewish neighbors. All residents were deported and killed during World War II.

St. Procopius Basilica, built in the 1240s–1260s in Romanesque-Gothic style for the Benedictine abbey founded in 1101, measures 72 meters long. Its crypt with 50 columns, each capital different, dates from 1220. It was consecrated in 1704 to St. Procopius on the 500th anniversary of his canonization.

activities.text: Walk through the maze of lanes in the Jewish Quarter, along the Jihlava River, then climb to the castle for views over the entire site.

days_plan.itin_title: The Most Beautiful Places in the Czech Republic

visits.text: One of the largest castle complexes in the country, Třeboň Castle unfolds the Rožmberks' Renaissance apartments and courtiers' chambers. You even learn the rules of period table etiquette. The park borders the Svět pond. Guided tour, vast estate that grew over centuries.

Masaryk Square lines up Renaissance and baroque gables, including the House at the White Stallion and the residence of Štěpánek Netolický, the engineer of the fishponds. Climb the town hall tower, 31 meters with an onion dome, for the view over the town and Svět pond. Modest ticket.

Hidden in the trees by the Svět, in Domanín, the neo-Gothic mausoleum of the Schwarzenbergs holds 26 family tombs from the line that ruled the region from 1660. Stained glass, crypt open on guided tour. Three kilometers from the center, on foot along the dike or by bike.

activities.text: Circle the world without leaving Třeboň: the Svět pond, 200 hectares, is ringed by a nature trail, beach to the north, Ostende tavern to the south. On foot or by bike, count on half a day for the big loop. The surrounding fishponds are a masterpiece of medieval hydraulics.

The Regent Brewery, founded in 1379, ranks among the oldest in the world and can be visited between two stone gate-towers on the south side of town. Tour of the historic rooms and tasting of unfiltered beer. Pair it with a portion of carp fries, local specialty.

If the road has compressed you, the peat bath at Třeboň Spa soothes the musculoskeletal system, a tradition over a century old. The spa also offers sauna and pools. Treatments must be booked in advance, often by prescription on the Czech side.

days_plan.itin_title: South Bohemia: Český Krumlov, Castles & Ponds in 10 Days

visits.text: Baba and Panna: two basalt pinnacles of 47 and 57 meters, christened the Old Woman and the Virgin. Trosky Castle, founded in the second half of the 14th century by Čeněk of Wartenberg, was built straddling these two pinnacles. Never conquered by the Hussites, it was burned by the Imperial army in 1648. Its ruins visible from across the region are the symbol of Bohemian Paradise.

activities.text: Climb up to Trosky's ruins in 20 minutes from the parking lot. The ascent of Panna (the high tower) is steep but doable. The view over the forests and surrounding rock cities justifies the effort. Open May-October according to schedules posted on site. Paid admission.

days_plan.itin_title: Northern Bohemia: From the Elbe to the Rocks of Bohemian Paradise
Bohemia: Prague, sandstone cities & thermal spas in 14 days

visits.text: Krakonoš, the guardian giant of the Giant Mountains, watches over the square that bears his name in Trutnov—a large bronze fountain in the center of a pleasant colonnaded space to walk through. The city calls itself the 'gateway to the Giant Mountains,' and the formula fits: it's the last major town before the mountains.

The cast-iron obelisk on Šibeník hill doesn't look like much from afar, but it carries historical weight: erected in 1868 to commemorate the Battle of Trutnov on June 27, 1866, Austria's only victory against Prussia during that war. Baron Ludwig von Gablenz himself had asked to be buried at the foot of this monument—done in 1874.

activities.text: Climb Šibeník by following the marked 4 km trail from Krakonoš Square, called 'In the Footsteps of the 1866 War': a dozen panels in Czech, German, and Polish punctuate the battle's progression. Count on 1h30 round trip from downtown.

days_plan.itin_title: Northeast Bohemia: From the Giant Mountains to the Rock Cities, 9 Days

visits.text: Valdštejn Castle, founded between 1260 and 1280 by the ancestors of the powerful eponymous family, is the oldest castle in Bohemian Paradise. After a 16th-century fire, the ruins were transformed in the 18th and 19th centuries into a Romantic pilgrimage site with a Baroque Chapel of St. John of Nepomuk and a stone bridge adorned with sculptures (1713). The cellars carved into the rock and the dungeons are accessible on the tour.

The Museum of Bohemian Paradise (Muzeum Českého ráje) in Turnov smells of varnish and old wood - its collections cover sandstone geology, local precious stones (Turnov was once a center for cutting garnets and gemstones), and regional history. It's also where you'll find the tourist office and hiking maps of the rock cities.

activities.text: From Turnov, walk on the Golden Trail of Bohemian Paradise toward Hrubá Skála (3.5 km, about 1h). This classic path crosses the sandstone forest with views of the rock towers. It also serves as a bike trail. You can make a loop by returning through the interior of the rock city.

days_plan.itin_title: Northern Bohemia: From the Elbe to the Rocks of Bohemian Paradise
Bohemia: Prague, sandstone cities & thermal spas in 14 days

visits.text: Wagner came here for inspiration, fascinated by the ruins of Střekov Castle perched on a black phonolite rock 100 meters above the Elbe. Built in 1316-1319 by John of Luxembourg to monitor navigation and collect passage fees, the castle is also associated with Goethe and the poet Karel Hynek Mácha. The Knights' Hall, the Gothic cylindrical tower, and the terrace with views over the Elbe valley are the highlights.

The 1998 Mariánský most bridge is beautiful but strangely discreet for a structure listed among the ten most beautiful constructions of the 1990s by Structural Engineering International magazine. From the bridge, Střekov Castle on its black rock is visible in full scope. Větruše Castle, accessible by 330-meter cable car from the Forum shopping center, offers a terrace with panoramic views over the city.

days_plan.itin_title: Northern Bohemia: From the Elbe to the Rocks of Bohemian Paradise

visits.text: Velká Amerika slices a turquoise gash into the limestone plateau near Mořina village. This abandoned limestone quarry, about half a mile long and up to 330 feet deep, flooded after extraction stopped: logical nickname, the "Czech Grand Canyon." Tunnels link it to other quarries with names like Malá Amerika or Mexiko. Descent to the bottom is forbidden and fined; stay on the overlooks above.

activities.text: If you like to walk, loop around the Velká Amerika quarry on the rim trail. The loop runs along the void and offers the best views of the blue water and white walls. Count a good hour, no real difficulty. Stay cautious near unsecured edges, and park along the road: a local often asks a few euros for the spot.

days_plan.itin_title: 7 Days Around Prague: Kutná Hora, Castles & Karst

visits.text: Minutes from the basilica, the Vojna Memorial chills the blood. This communist labor camp imprisoned opponents of the regime, often forced to work the uranium mines nearby. Barracks and watchtowers have been preserved. It's the brutal underside of the Baroque postcard, to be seen to understand 20th-century Czech history. Visit rather in season; check opening before making the detour.

days_plan.itin_title: 7 Days Around Prague: Kutná Hora, Castles & Karst

visits.text: The air changes here. The Elbe is now just a torrent and the first peaks of the Krkonoše close the northern horizon. Vrchlabí presents itself as the gateway to the Giant Mountains: a quiet small town with a Great Square and a Renaissance château you can walk around in twenty minutes.

The Krkonoše Museum, reopened in 2023 after a lengthy renovation, is the best reason to stop in Vrchlabí. Housed in a former Augustinian convent, it traces the geological and human history of the Krkonoše with polished multimedia installations. The ticket is valid for two days at all national park museums.

activities.text: Spend twenty minutes in the park of Vrchlabí château, headquarters of the KRNAP national park administration: the courtyard is freely accessible, even though the interior isn't open for visits. The old linden trees and the parapet overlooking the river make it a pleasant stop.

days_plan.itin_title: Northeast Bohemia: From the Giant Mountains to the Rock Cities, 9 Days

visits.text: Founded in 1259 by Vok of Rožmberk, reportedly in gratitude for escaping drowning in the Vltava, the Cistercian monastery of Vyšší Brod still houses monks. Its rococo library of 70,000 volumes is the third-largest in Czechia. The Záviš Cross is kept here—70 cm of gold and gems—and the Rožmberk tomb beneath the choir. Guided tour, sometimes an organ resounding beneath the vaults.

activities.text: At the edge of the Šumava, the monastery opens onto the trails: hiking and cycling paths lead toward Lake Lipno, and the Vltava can be descended by canoe to Český Krumlov. Café, shop, and postal museum on-site before setting off.

days_plan.itin_title: South Bohemia: Český Krumlov, Castles & Ponds in 10 Days

places.name: Zelezna Ruda

visits.text: Šumava National Park, often called 'the green heart of Europe,' stretches 80 km along the Bavarian border. A UNESCO Biosphere Reserve since 1990, it harbors five glacial lakes, centuries-old primeval forests, peat bogs, and populations of lynx, wolf, and capercaillie. During the Cold War, the region was long a military zone closed to the public, which allowed nature to remain virtually pristine.

Černé jezero (Black Lake) is the Czech Republic's largest and deepest natural lake (40 meters deep). Its black color comes from the reflection of the surrounding spruce forests. One kilometer away, Čertovo jezero (Devil's Lake), with clear, transparent waters, owes its name to a legend where the devil, dragged to the bottom by a girl cleverer than him, is said to have carved this valley. By geographical curiosity, the two lakes separated by a few hundred meters drain into different basins: Černé jezero toward the North Sea, Čertovo jezero toward the Black Sea.

activities.text: Hike to both glacial lakes from Špičácké sedlo parking lot. The yellow-marked trail descends in 1h to Čertovo and Černé jezero lakes (8.3 km loop, 328 m elevation gain).
In the evening, cycle on forest trails in the park from Železná Ruda—several rental shops offer e-bikes for marked trails around the village.

days_plan.itin_title: The Most Beautiful Places in the Czech Republic

map_keywords: Černé jezero Lake Šumava Black Lake
Čertovo jezero Lake Šumava Devil's Lake

visits.text: On a spur the Vltava wraps around on three sides, the Cistercian monastery of Zlatá Koruna has kept its medieval atmosphere intact. Founded in 1263 by Přemysl Otakar II, it shelters the rare two-level Gothic Chapel of the Guardian Angels and the largest church in South Bohemia. Guided tour April to October, last one starts an hour before closing. French text available.

activities.text: Walk along the Vltava at the foot of the monastery: the valley floor, calm and wooded, lends itself to a short walk between Zlatá Koruna and the meanders. A few easy kilometers, perfect for digesting the road. Spot the ferry and jetties depending on the season.
